free_text: Excitation function for alpha-particle-induced
reactions in light-mass nuclei
Stacks of self-supporting metallic foils
Beam currents of the order of 250 nano-Amperes
for vanadium stacks and 450 nano-Amperes for cobalt
stacks were maintained for 3 and 2 hours, respectively.
60-MeV diffused beam.
- flux monitor
Compiler believes that ref. given in the article is
wrong (JIN,42,937,1980 - reaction (He3,2n) is given).
95 cm3 Ge(Li) and 50 cm3 HPGe detectors
having a resolution of 2 keV for 1332 keV photons.
The energy and efficiency calibration of the detectors
were performed by using a standard 152Eu source
The overall error in the cross-section
measurement is less than 15%, which composed of:
Uncertainty in the photopeak area.
Uncertainty in the detection efficiency.
Uncertainty in the foil thickness.
Uncertainty in the spectroscopic
data.
Uncertainty in the flux monitor
Natural vanadium and cobalt, purity better 99.99%)
was used for making the targets. See Method also.
Preequilibrium Model. Code ALICE/85/300.
By F.C.
DATE is corrected
Correction in MONIT-REF was made,
according M.Bossant comment -SB
SD: Author's name and Institute code
corrected. BIB updated. Small corrections in all
Subents. MONIT-ERR -> ERR-5. MONIT-REF corrected.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.

99.99% and thickness 8 mg/cm2 were interspersed with
aluminium foils of suitable thickness acting as
energy degrader.
